text: Entering this modern, glass-walled space, I am struck by a sense of curiosity and anticipation. There is a cold sterility to the architecture, offset by the blue and purple lights that beckon from within the meeting room. The fire safety signage and equipment remind me of the structure and routines that underpin even the most innovative environments. As I step closer and observe the R+D display, I feel a stimulating mix of wonder and introspection. The exhibit invites me to reflect on human creativity and our relationship with technology. Here, I am both an observer and a participant—conscious of the boundaries between my inner thoughts and the world of research and development that surrounds me. I feel thoughtful, almost contemplative, eager to engage yet aware of my own smallness within this engineered landscape.
